title: Right of Option
description: What is the right of option at Pôle Emploi and under what conditions can an unemployed person choose to switch from lower to higher unemployment benefits?
narrative: The user is an unemployed individual in France who has previously held multiple jobs with varying levels of remuneration and is now eligible to exercise the 'right of option' offered by Pôle Emploi. This mechanism allows them to forfeit remaining lower-value unemployment benefits in exchange for higher daily allowances from a more recent, better-paid job — but only if specific conditions are met: the new allowance must be at least 30% higher than the old one, the user must have worked at least four months since their last unemployment period, and the remaining old benefits must not yet be exhausted. The user needs to understand the eligibility criteria, the formal application process (including the 21-day cooling-off period), and the trade-offs involved — namely, the permanent loss of previously accrued rights in exchange for larger, but shorter-term, payments. They may be considering starting a business or returning to work soon, making the timing and financial impact of this choice critical. Relevance is determined by documents that clearly explain the legal conditions, procedural steps, financial implications, and personal suitability of exercising this option, excluding general unemployment simulations, employer attestations, or administrative procedures unrelated to benefit selection.
